page.content: In the study by Simpson et al. 2000, the total number of patients with DR was 16, with 4 combined DR and head injury patients, yielding a co-existence percentage of 25.0%. Chen et al. 1991 reported 62 total patients with DR, 3 of whom had a head injury, resulting in a 4.8% co-existence rate.

cells.cell_text: Total number of patient with DR
Combined DR and head injury patients

page.content: Pfannschmidt et al. 1994 recorded a total of 58 patients with DR, with 22 combined cases of head injury, leading to a co-existence percentage of 37.9%. Balci et al. 2004 had 137 total patients with DR, 33 of whom also had a head injury, resulting in a 24.0% co-existence rate. Lastly, Ilgenfritz et al. 1992 documented 52 patients with DR, with 21 combined patients, producing a co-existence percentage of 40.3%. In Table 2, wash number 1 utilized water as a solvent with [C2mim][OAc] content of 43.43 ± 0.72% (w/v), wash number 2 also used water with 7.56 ± 0.15%, wash number 3 utilized water with 2.06 ± 0.09%, wash number 4 employed water with 0.48 ± 0.02%, wash number 5 used ethanol with 0.20 ± 0.01%, wash number 6 combined ethanol and water with 0.12 ± 0.01%, wash number 7 also combined ethanol and water with 0.06 ± 0.00%, and wash number 8 used water with 0.03 ± 0.00%.
